allow: Bash(gemini -p "Research the Palit GeForce RTX 3090 GamingPro graphics card specifications. I need exact physical dimensions: length in mm, width in slots/mm, height in mm. Also power connectors \(type and number\), TDP/power consumption at load, and boost clock speed in MHz stock. Please search for official Palit specifications and reviews from TechPowerUp, Tom's Hardware, Hardware Unboxed. Provide specific measurements.")

Bash(gemini -p "Research the Palit GeForce RTX 3090 GamingPro graphics card. I need the following specific information:
1. Physical dimensions: exact length in mm, slot count \(how many PCIe slots wide\), height in mm
2. Power connectors: number and type \(8-pin, 16-pin/12VHPWR, etc.\)
3. TDP and total power consumption at load
4. Boost clock speed in MHz \(stock\)
5. Cooling solution: number of fans, fan size, heatsink design details
Please cite specific sources like TechPowerUp, Tom's Hardware, Palit official specs page. Include URLs.")

Bash(gemini -p "Compare Ollama vs llama.cpp server vs vLLM vs LM Studio headless for running local LLMs on Ubuntu 24.04 server in 2025/2026. For each, cover: 1\) Ease of setup on headless Ubuntu \(no GUI\), 2\) OpenAI-compatible REST API support, 3\) Performance for coding tasks, 4\) Support for coding models like Qwen2.5-Coder, DeepSeek-Coder-V2, CodeLlama, 5\) Multi-client support over network, 6\) GPU utilization and memory management. Include specific version numbers, GitHub URLs, and any key limitations. Which is best for a homelab server accessed by multiple clients over VPN?")

Bash(gemini -p "Research AI agent frameworks for local LLMs that provide tool use capabilities \(file read/write, code execution, PDF parsing\) in 2025/2026. Cover these specifically: 1\) Open Interpreter - can it run in headless server/API mode? Does it expose an API other clients can call? What's its current status and GitHub URL? 2\) Aider - does it support local models via Ollama/llama.cpp? What's its architecture - is it a CLI tool only or can it run as a server? 3\) smolagents by HuggingFace - what tools does it include out of the box? Does it support local LLMs? 4\) LangChain/LangGraph agents - how do you build a coding agent with file tools? Include GitHub links, version numbers, and practical assessment of which is best for building a Claude Code equivalent.")

Bash(gemini -p "Research web UIs for local AI models in 2025/2026, specifically focusing on tool use, function calling, file system access, and code execution capabilities. Cover: 1\) Open WebUI - does it support tool use/function calling? File uploads with content reading? Code execution? Plugins/tools architecture? 2\) LibreChat - agent capabilities, tool use support, file handling? 3\) Lobe Chat - tool use and file system access? 4\) Any other web UIs that allow building agents with real tool execution \(not just code display\)? For each: GitHub URL, current version, whether it's a pure chat UI or can execute actions. Which web UIs are genuinely agentic vs just pretty chat interfaces?")

Bash(gemini -p "Research the best coding LLMs available in 2025/2026 that run on consumer GPUs with 8-24GB VRAM for local deployment. Focus on models good at: 1\) Tool use and function calling, 2\) Code generation and debugging, 3\) Following complex multi-step instructions. Cover: Qwen2.5-Coder series \(sizes, quantization, performance\), DeepSeek-Coder-V2 and DeepSeek-R1 distills, CodeLlama latest, Codestral by Mistral, Phi-4 by Microsoft, any 2025/2026 releases. For each: VRAM requirements at different quantizations \(Q4, Q5, Q8\), tokens per second on RTX 3090/4090, benchmark scores \(HumanEval, MBPP\), Ollama/HuggingFace availability. What's the best model for a coding assistant with tool use on a single RTX 4090 \(24GB VRAM\)?")

Bash(gemini -p "Research security considerations for running a local AI agent with code execution and file system access on an Ubuntu 24.04 server in 2025/2026. The setup is: AI server accessible over Tailscale VPN to trusted clients, AI can read/write files and execute Python scripts. Cover: 1\) Sandboxing/containerization for code execution \(Docker, Firejail, systemd sandboxing\), 2\) File system permission boundaries - how to limit AI access to specific directories, 3\) Code execution risks - what malicious code could an AI generate/run and how to mitigate, 4\) Network access controls for executed code, 5\) How Open Interpreter and similar tools handle security, 6\) Whether to run the AI agent as a dedicated low-privilege user, 7\) Logging and audit trails for AI actions. Practical recommendations for a homelab context where security matters but we're not running enterprise infrastructure.")

Bash(gemini -p "What are the best locally-runnable coding LLMs in early 2026 for a system with a single RTX 4090 \(24GB VRAM\)? Focus on: 1\) Qwen2.5-Coder series - what sizes exist \(7B, 14B, 32B, 72B\), which fit in 24GB VRAM at what quantization, HumanEval benchmark scores; 2\) DeepSeek-R1 distilled models - coding capability, VRAM requirements; 3\) Codestral by Mistral - is it open for local use? 4\) Phi-4 by Microsoft - coding capability, VRAM needs; 5\) Llama 3.3 70B - tool use capability; 6\) Any new coding-focused models released in late 2025 or early 2026. For each model list: parameter count, minimum VRAM for Q4 quantization, approximate tokens/second on RTX 4090, and whether it supports function calling/tool use natively. Focus on practical homelab use.")

Bash(gemini -p "Research the recommended architecture for a 'local Claude Code equivalent' setup in early 2026. The goal is: headless Ubuntu server running AI models, clients connect via Tailscale VPN, AI can read/write files, read PDFs, execute Python. What is the recommended 'stack' combining: model server \(Ollama/vLLM\), agent framework \(Aider/Open Interpreter/custom\), and optionally a web UI? Specifically: 1\) Is the combination of Ollama + Aider the simplest working setup for a Claude Code equivalent via CLI? Walk through how that actually works end-to-end. 2\) Is the combination of Ollama + Open WebUI + Pipelines a good web browser approach? 3\) What does MCP \(Model Context Protocol\) add to this stack and is it mature enough to use in 2026? 4\) For PDF reading specifically, what tool or approach works best within these frameworks? 5\) What's the minimum viable setup to get started quickly, and what does the 'scaled up' version look like?")

Bash(gemini -p "Search for 'Qwen-Image-Edit-2511 ComfyUI' - are there any custom nodes, workflow JSONs, or integration guides for using the Hugging Face model Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit-2511 in ComfyUI? Include any GitHub repositories, Reddit posts, or forum discussions you can find. Also search for 'QwenImageEditPlus ComfyUI' as that is the pipeline class name for this model.")

Bash(gemini -p "Search Hugging Face for any Comfy-Org repackaged version of the Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit-2511 model. Check if there is a repository at 'Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image-Edit-2511' or similar names like 'Comfy-Org/Qwen_Image_Edit_ComfyUI_Repackaged' on HuggingFace. Also search for any community HuggingFace repos that have adapted this model for ComfyUI use.")

Bash(gemini -p "Search GitHub for any issues or pull requests in the ComfyUI repository \(comfyanonymous/ComfyUI\) mentioning 'Qwen-Image-Edit' or 'QwenImageEdit'. Also search GitHub more broadly for any repositories or code that implements ComfyUI support for Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit-2511. Include any custom node repositories that mention this model.")

Bash(gemini -p "Search for the GitHub pull request history showing when native Qwen-Image-Edit support was added to ComfyUI \(comfyanonymous/ComfyUI\). Specifically: when was the first PR merged that added support for Qwen-Image-Edit models? What nodes does native ComfyUI provide for these models? Is Qwen-Image-Edit-2511 supported natively \(not requiring custom nodes\) in ComfyUI as of early 2026? Search for commits or PRs in the range #10000-#12000 of comfyanonymous/ComfyUI.")

Bash(gemini -p "Research AI applications and platforms for running local AI workloads on Linux, specifically for a user with dual NVIDIA RTX 3090 GPUs \(2x 24GB VRAM = 48GB total\). Please provide comprehensive, current information as of early 2026 covering:

1. LM Studio - capabilities, Linux support, multi-GPU support, pros/cons, active development status
2. AUTOMATIC1111 \(stable-diffusion-webui\) - capabilities, Linux support, multi-GPU support, active development status, comparison to ComfyUI
3. ComfyUI - multi-GPU support status \(can it use 2 GPUs simultaneously?\), any workarounds or upcoming features
4. Ollama - multi-GPU support, how it handles multiple GPUs, limitations
5. Open WebUI - what it does, relationship to Ollama, Linux support, features
6. lllyasviel's Forge \(stable-diffusion-webui-forge\) - what it is, how it differs from A1111, multi-GPU support
7. InvokeAI - capabilities, Linux support, multi-GPU, development status
8. Fooocus - capabilities, Linux support, development status \(note: lllyasviel archived this repo\)
9. Jan.ai - capabilities, Linux support, multi-GPU support
10. AnythingLLM - capabilities, Linux support, features
11. Text Generation WebUI \(oobabooga\) - capabilities, Linux support, multi-GPU support, development status
12. vLLM - what it is, capabilities, Linux support, multi-GPU support \(key differentiator\)
13. llama.cpp - capabilities, Linux support, multi-GPU support

For each, please research: what it does \(LLM? Image gen? Video? Multi-modal?\), Linux support quality, multi-GPU capabilities for dual RTX 3090, active development status in 2025/2026, ease of use \(GUI vs CLI\), key strengths and weaknesses.

Focus on: which tools can genuinely take advantage of dual GPU setups, which are actively maintained, and which offer capabilities beyond what Ollama + ComfyUI provide." 2>&1 | head -300)

Bash(gemini -p "Research vLLM for local AI inference on Linux with multiple NVIDIA GPUs. Specifically:
1. What is vLLM - is it a GUI application, CLI tool, or server/API?
2. How does vLLM handle multi-GPU inference - does it support tensor parallelism across 2x RTX 3090?
3. What is the performance difference between vLLM and Ollama for LLM inference?
4. What models does vLLM support - is it compatible with GGUF models like Ollama uses?
5. Installation method on Linux - pip, Docker, native?
6. Is vLLM actively maintained as of 2025/2026?
7. What are the key advantages of vLLM over Ollama for a power user?
8. Does vLLM have a web UI or is it purely API/CLI?
9. Can vLLM be used as a backend with Open WebUI as a frontend?
10. Memory requirements and VRAM usage compared to Ollama

Please provide specific technical details and cite sources where possible." 2>&1)

Bash(gemini -p "Research Text Generation WebUI \(oobabooga\) and llama.cpp for local LLM inference on Linux with dual NVIDIA GPUs \(2x RTX 3090, 48GB VRAM total\).

For Text Generation WebUI \(oobabooga/text-generation-webui\):
1. Current development status - is it still actively maintained in 2025/2026?
2. Multi-GPU support - can it split a model across 2x RTX 3090?
3. What backends does it support \(llama.cpp, ExLlamaV2, Transformers\)?
4. UI quality - is it good for non-technical users?
5. Key features beyond what Ollama provides \(system prompts, characters, notebooks, etc.\)
6. GGUF model support
7. Installation on Linux

For llama.cpp:
1. What is llama.cpp - CLI tool or library?
2. Multi-GPU support with CUDA - how does it handle 2 GPUs?
3. Performance compared to Ollama \(which uses llama.cpp internally\)
4. GGUF model support
5. Is it a backend that Ollama and others build on?
6. Any advantages of using llama.cpp directly vs through Ollama?

Also research: ExLlamaV2 - what is it, how does it relate to Text Generation WebUI, performance advantages for RTX 3090 hardware.

Please provide current information as of early 2026." 2>&1)

Bash(gemini -p "Research Text Generation WebUI \(oobabooga/text-generation-webui\) and llama.cpp for local LLM inference on Linux with dual NVIDIA GPUs \(2x RTX 3090, 48GB VRAM total\). As of early 2026:

For Text Generation WebUI \(oobabooga\):
1. Is it still actively maintained in 2025/2026?
2. Multi-GPU support - can it split a large model across 2x RTX 3090?
3. What backends does it support \(llama.cpp, ExLlamaV2, Transformers, etc.\)?
4. Key features beyond Ollama \(characters/personas, notebooks, extensions, API modes\)
5. GGUF and GPTQ model support
6. Ease of use for non-technical users - is it GUI or CLI?
7. Linux installation method

For llama.cpp:
1. What is it - CLI tool, library, or server?
2. Multi-GPU CUDA support - how does it handle 2 GPUs? Flag used?
3. Performance vs Ollama \(Ollama wraps llama.cpp\)
4. GGUF model support
5. Any advantages of using llama.cpp directly vs through Ollama?

For ExLlamaV2:
1. What is it and what makes it different?
2. Performance advantages on RTX 3090 \(Ampere\)?
3. EXL2 quantization format - how does it compare to GGUF?
4. Is it used as a backend in other tools?

Please give concrete technical details." 2>&1)

Bash(gemini -p "Research these AI platforms for local use on Linux with dual NVIDIA RTX 3090 GPUs \(48GB VRAM total\) - provide current information as of early 2026:

1. Open WebUI - what is it \(frontend for Ollama/OpenAI API?\), features, Linux support, multi-GPU relevance, active development status

2. Jan.ai - what is it \(Ollama alternative? Desktop app?\), Linux support, multi-GPU support, model compatibility, development status 2025/2026

3. AnythingLLM - what is it \(document chat? RAG?\), Linux support, Docker available?, multi-GPU, key differentiator from Ollama

4. LM Studio - what is it, Linux support status \(was there a Linux beta?\), multi-GPU support on Linux, pros/cons vs Ollama

5. lllyasviel's Forge \(stable-diffusion-webui-forge\) - how does it differ from AUTOMATIC1111, does it support multi-GPU for image generation with 2x RTX 3090, development status, Linux support

6. InvokeAI - what makes it different from ComfyUI/A1111, Linux support, multi-GPU support, GUI quality, development status 2025/2026

For each: what type of AI workload does it serve \(LLM? Image gen? Both? RAG/documents?\), ease of use, and whether it adds genuine value to a user who already has Ollama + ComfyUI." 2>&1)

Bash(gemini -p "Research these image generation and multi-modal AI platforms for Linux as of early 2026:

1. AUTOMATIC1111 \(stable-diffusion-webui\) - development status 2025/2026 \(is it still actively maintained or has development slowed?\), multi-GPU support for image generation across 2 GPUs, Linux installation, how it compares to ComfyUI for workflow flexibility

2. ComfyUI multi-GPU - current status \(can ComfyUI use 2 GPUs simultaneously for image/video generation?\), any plans for multi-GPU support, community workarounds

3. Fooocus \(lllyasviel\) - was this project archived by the developer? Current status 2025/2026, who maintains it now?

4. What AI image/video generation platforms support genuine multi-GPU acceleration for consumer grade GPUs \(RTX 3090\) on Linux? Are there ANY image generation tools that can split generation across 2 GPUs?

5. Ollama multi-GPU - how exactly does Ollama handle multiple GPUs? Does it do tensor parallelism or just load on one GPU? Does it support distributing a model across 2x RTX 3090?

Please provide specific technical details and current GitHub activity status where possible." 2>&1)

Bash(gemini -p "Research local RAG \(Retrieval-Augmented Generation\) setup on Ubuntu Linux with Ollama and Open WebUI, focusing on PostgreSQL with pgvector as the vector database. Please cover:

1. pgvector PostgreSQL extension: What is it, installation on Ubuntu 22.04/24.04, pros/cons vs alternatives \(Chroma, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus\) for local RAG
2. Open WebUI built-in RAG: Does Open WebUI have native document upload and RAG? What vector backends does it support? Does it natively support pgvector?
3. Main RAG pipeline approaches with Ollama:
   - Open WebUI built-in RAG
   - External frameworks: LangChain, LlamaIndex, Haystack, AnythingLLM with Ollama + pgvector
   - Custom pipeline approach
4. AnythingLLM: Does it support Ollama as the LLM backend? Does it support PostgreSQL/pgvector as vector DB?
5. Best embedding models available via Ollama \(e.g. nomic-embed-text, mxbai-embed-large, all-minilm\) for RAG - dimensions, performance, VRAM requirements
6. Specific configuration steps for the recommended approach

Hardware context: Ubuntu bare metal, dual RTX 3090 \(48GB VRAM\), Ollama installed, Open WebUI in Docker

Provide specific version numbers, commands, and configuration details. Focus on what is actually supported vs what requires custom code.")

Bash(gemini -p "What is pgvector? How do you install it on Ubuntu 22.04 or 24.04 with PostgreSQL 16? What are the key SQL commands to enable it and create a vector column? Compare pgvector vs Chroma vs Qdrant vs Weaviate for local RAG - pros, cons, ease of setup. Is pgvector production-ready for local RAG in 2025?")

Bash(gemini -p "Does Open WebUI \(the Docker-based Ollama frontend\) have built-in RAG document upload support? What vector databases does Open WebUI natively support? Does it support pgvector or PostgreSQL as a vector store? What are the Open WebUI environment variables needed to configure RAG? What embedding models can it use?")

Bash(gemini -p "What are the best Ollama embedding models for RAG in 2025? Compare: nomic-embed-text, mxbai-embed-large, all-minilm, bge-m3, snowflake-arctic-embed. Include: embedding dimensions, model size in GB, MTEB benchmark scores, tokens per second performance, and recommended use cases. Which is best for general document RAG on a system with 48GB VRAM running Ollama?")

Bash(gemini -p "Does AnythingLLM support Ollama as the LLM backend and pgvector/PostgreSQL as the vector database? How does AnythingLLM compare to Open WebUI built-in RAG for document management? What are the pros and cons of each for a local RAG setup? What is the Docker installation command for AnythingLLM? What are its system requirements?")

Bash(gemini -p "AnythingLLM local RAG system: Does it support Ollama as the LLM backend? Does it support PostgreSQL with pgvector as vector database? Provide the Docker run command to install AnythingLLM. Compare AnythingLLM vs Open WebUI for document RAG management - which is better for a non-developer user wanting to chat with their own documents? What are the limitations of each?")

Bash(gemini -p "Provide a step-by-step setup guide for using Open WebUI with pgvector \(PostgreSQL\) and Ollama embedding models for RAG on Ubuntu Linux. Include: 1\) PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector installation commands, 2\) The exact docker-compose.yml or docker run command for Open WebUI with VECTOR_DB=pgvector environment variables, 3\) How to configure Open WebUI to use Ollama for embeddings \(nomic-embed-text\), 4\) How to test that RAG is working. Be very specific with commands and config values.")

Bash(gemini -p "Search for ComfyUI custom nodes that support Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit model from Alibaba on HuggingFace. This model uses QwenImageEditPipeline from the diffusers library. I need to know: 1\) Is there a ComfyUI custom node that natively supports this model? 2\) What is the GitHub repository URL and node name? 3\) How do you load it in ComfyUI - what directory do model files go in? Search GitHub repos, ComfyUI registry at registry.comfy.org, and ComfyUI community forums. Return the most current information available.")

Bash(gemini -p "Search GitHub for ComfyUI nodes for Qwen2.5-VL image editing or Qwen image editing pipeline. Specifically look for: 1\) Any repo with 'comfyui' AND 'qwen' AND 'image edit' in the name or description 2\) The diffusers QwenImageEditPipeline - what models does it support and what HuggingFace model IDs use it? 3\) Is Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit the same as any Qwen2.5 model variant? Provide GitHub URLs if found.")

Bash(gemini -p "Look up the HuggingFace model page for Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit. What is this model? What is its architecture? What diffusers pipeline class does it use \(QwenImageEditPipeline\)? Is it part of the Qwen2.5 family or a separate model? What are the model file sizes and what files are in the repo? Also search for any ComfyUI integration examples or notebooks that use this specific model.")

Bash(gemini -p "Search GitHub for 'ComfyUI Qwen-Image-Edit' nodes. I need specific, verified results. Look for: 1\) Any Comfy-Org official support for Qwen-Image-Edit \(check github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI_frontend or comfyanonymous/ComfyUI for native node support\) 2\) The AIFSH/QwenImage-ComfyUI repository - what are the exact node names and model file requirements? 3\) Any other well-maintained repo with stars on GitHub for Qwen-Image-Edit in ComfyUI. Also: does ComfyUI natively support QwenImageEditPipeline without custom nodes?")
Bash(curl -s "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AIFSH/QwenImage-ComfyUI/main/__init__.py" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "\(NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS|NODE_DISPLAY_NAME|class Load|class Qwen|class Run\)" | head -40)

Bash(curl -s "https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q=ComfyUI+Qwen+Image+Edit&sort=stars&order=desc" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; data=json.load\(sys.stdin\); [print\(f\\"{r['full_name']} | stars:{r['stargazers_count']} | {r['html_url']} | {r['description']}\\"\) for r in data.get\('items',[]\)[:10]]")
Bash(curl -s "https://api.github.com/repos/lenML/comfyui_qwen_image_edit_adv/contents/" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; [print\(f['name']\) for f in json.load\(sys.stdin\)]")

Bash(curl -s "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/princepainter/ComfyUI-PainterQwenImageEdit/main/PainterQwenImageEditPlus.py" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "\(NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS|NODE_DISPLAY|class Painter|class Load|class Qwen\)" | head -20)

Bash(curl -s "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lenML/comfyui_qwen_image_edit_adv/main/nodes_edit.py" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "\(NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS|class |RETURN_TYPES|CATEGORY\)" | head -30 && echo "---" && curl -s "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lenML/comfyui_qwen_image_edit_adv/main/nodes_scale.py" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "\(NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS|class \)" | head -10)

Bash(gemini -p "Search for the GitHub repository lenML/comfyui_qwen_image_edit_adv README. I need: 1\) The exact model files required, 2\) Which HuggingFace repos to download from \(exact model IDs like Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit\), 3\) Where each file goes in ComfyUI directory structure \(e.g. models/vae/, models/text_encoders/, etc\). Please provide the specific download instructions as listed in the README.")

Bash(gemini -p "What is Qwen-Image-Edit-Lightning? Is it a distilled faster version of Qwen-Image-Edit? Who made it - Alibaba/Qwen team or a third party? What are the trade-offs vs the full model? Is it suitable for general image editing tasks or does it sacrifice quality significantly? Are there HuggingFace repos for it? Please provide specific HuggingFace model IDs if they exist.")

Bash(gemini -p "For the HuggingFace repository Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit, which folders contain actual model weight files \(.safetensors or .bin files\) vs configuration files only? Specifically tell me about: processor/, scheduler/, tokenizer/, transformer/, vae/, text_encoder/ - which contain weights and what are the file sizes? Is the transformer folder the main model weights? Is vae/ a separate VAE component? What is in text_encoder/ - is it a CLIP or T5 encoder?")

Bash(gemini -p "Is there a HuggingFace repository that provides fp8 or quantized versions of ALL THREE components of Qwen-Image-Edit \(transformer weights + VAE + text encoder\) packaged together with clear ComfyUI directory placement instructions? Similar to how Comfy-Org packages Wan2.2 models. Search for repos like: Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image-Edit, any fp8 quantized Qwen image edit repos, or community-packaged versions. Provide exact HuggingFace model IDs and file names.")

Bash(gemini -p "Find the GitHub repository lenML/comfyui_qwen_image_edit_adv. What model files does it require? Specifically: which HuggingFace repos to download from, what the exact filenames are, and where do they go in ComfyUI's models/ directory structure? Look for information about its node names and the ComfyUI model directory paths it uses.")

Bash(gemini -p "Find information about Qwen-Image-Edit-Lightning on HuggingFace. Is it a distilled or accelerated version of Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit? Who published it? What are its trade-offs vs the full model? Search for HuggingFace repos matching 'Qwen-Image-Edit-Lightning' and provide the exact model IDs. Also search for any fp8 quantized versions of Qwen-Image-Edit transformer weights on HuggingFace.")
